title: Athanasia adenantha
taxonomy: Athanasia adenantha is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is due to its shared characteristics with other members of the Asteraceae family, such as its composite flower structure and the presence of achenes. The genus Athanasia is native to South Africa, and Athanasia adenantha is one of the species within this genus. The species is further classified within the tribe Gnaphalieae, a subfamily of the Asteraceae family characterized by its unique inflorescence and foliage. This classification system helps to organize and understand the relationships between different plant species and their evolutionary history.

intro: Athanasia adenantha, also known as Cape chamomile or Eriocephalus punctatus, is a plant species native to South Africa, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The health benefits of Athanasia adenantha include its ability to calm the nervous system, reduce anxiety and insomnia, and alleviate symptoms of depression, making it a popular herbal remedy for stress relief. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Athanasia adenantha include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and soothe digestive issues.
